title: Train a monotone-constrained mechanistic booster as a low-variance expert
summary: Your best solutions already compute mechanistic variables like service pressure, finish pressure, and finish-feasibility gaps.
rationale: Your best solutions already compute mechanistic variables like service pressure, finish pressure, and finish-feasibility gaps. Public booster docs support monotonic constraints, which can regularize these features so the model cannot learn non-physical inversions in sparse race regimes.
implementation_hint: Fit a separate LightGBM/XGBoost model only on interpretable numeric strategy features and enforce increasing constraints on stop-pressure variables and decreasing constraints on remaining-margin / cooldown variables. Blend its OOF ranks with the unconstrained main model rather than replacing the main booster.
expected_effect: Usually a small gain, but often improves robustness on held-out races and unseen strategy regimes by reducing noisy local inversions.
risk: Wrong sign assumptions can suppress real overcut or track-position effects; constrained hist trees may need larger bins or shallower tuning changes.
